description: Axis 1 (LOINC Component): the analyte or entity being measured. Examples: 'Glucose', 'Hemoglobin A1c', 'RNA expression', 'DNA methylation', 'Genotype call', 'Protein abundance', 'Microbial 16S rRNA'.
Vocabulary used for AssayAnalyteCode. LOINC where the analyte exists in LOINC; OBI or EFO for omics analytes LOINC does not model.
Identifier in AssayAnalyteVocabulary. Example: 'LOINC:2345-7' (Glucose mass concentration), 'OBI:0001271' (RNA-seq assay), 'HGNC:6893' (MAPT gene for transcript-level reporting).
Axis 2 (LOINC Property): kind of quantity being reported. Use LOINC property codes when applicable; RoP-extended values are documented for omics-specific quantities LOINC does not codify.

metadata_.extension_rationale: LOINC property axis does not currently codify omics-specific quantity types (read counts, fold change, variant allele frequency, methylation beta values). Extensions documented for transparency and proposed for upstream LOINC submission.

description: Axis 3 (LOINC Time): time aspect of the measurement. Almost always 'Pt' (point in time) for omics assays.

Axis 4 (LOINC System): the specimen/system measured. Bridges to RoP biosample anchors rather than duplicating that vocabulary — value should resolve to the BiosampleID and inherit its SampleTypeControlled.
Axis 5 (LOINC Scale): the scale of result reported. LOINC-native values used unchanged.

Axis 6 (LOINC Method): the assay technology category. Use LOINC method values where they exist; fall back to OBI for finer-grained omics methods. Platform-specific detail (vendor, model, kit) lives in the Platform sub-block CDEs that extend this axis.

When the full six-axis combination resolves to an existing LOINC code, populate it directly. Forge attempts auto-resolution from the six-axis tuple. Examples: '51969-4' (Genetic analysis report), '100124-9' (Genotyping for human disease panel), '75614-8' (Whole genome sequence report).

metadata_.derivation: Forge attempts six-axis to LOINC code lookup; populates when unambiguous match exists

description: Platform sub-block extending Axis 6 (Method). Vendor of the assay instrument or platform.

metadata_.rationale: LOINC method axis does not encode vendor at this granularity; required for batch-effect correction and vendor-specific QC pipelines.

description: Specific instrument model. Free text recommended, controlled values offered as preferred when applicable.

metadata_.preferred_examples: NovaSeq-6000, NovaSeq-X-Plus, NextSeq-2000, MiSeq, Chromium-X, Chromium-iX, PromethION-24, MinION-Mk1C, Revio, Sequel-IIe, Orbitrap-Astral, timsTOF-Pro

description: Library prep, capture, or chemistry kit used. Free text capturing what was actually recorded. Examples: 'TruSeq Stranded mRNA', 'Single Cell 3' v3.1', 'Twist Comprehensive Exome', 'Infinium MethylationEPIC v2.0'.
Version string for the kit/chemistry, when distinct from KitChemistry value. Examples: 'v1.5', '4.1', '2024-Q3'.

Cohort-scoped batch identifier for the assay run. Required for batch-effect correction in cross-cohort meta-analysis. Granularity is study-defined (sequencing run, plate, library prep batch) but must be consistent within a collection.

metadata_.rationale: Without batch granularity at the row level, post-hoc batch-effect modeling (ComBat, RUV, mixed-effects) is infeasible.

description: Reference assembly or panel used for genomic data alignment, variant calling, or quantification. Required when AssayAnalyteCode points to genomic content.

metadata_.notes: GRCh38 and hg38 differ only in chromosome naming (chr1 vs 1); record as collected; Forge normalizes downstream.

description: Bioinformatics pipeline or analysis software that produced the values reported in this row. Free text + version recommended. Examples: 'DRAGEN', 'GATK4-HaplotypeCaller', 'CellRanger', 'DeepVariant', 'STAR', 'Salmon', 'MaxQuant'.
Version string for PipelineSoftware. Required for reproducibility.

Source code repository URL for the analysis pipeline. Git URL preferred; container registry URL acceptable when the entire pipeline is distributed as a container without separate source. Examples: 'https://github.com/datatecnica/rop-pipeline', 'https://github.com/nf-core/rnaseq', 'oci://nfcore/rnaseq'. Secondary to PipelineSoftware/PipelineSoftwareVersion — those name what was run, this names where the code lives.

metadata_.rationale: Pipeline name + version is enough to identify well-known tools (DRAGEN, GATK4); for in-house or modified pipelines the source repository is the only durable reference.

description: Specific commit SHA (full 40-character preferred) or container image digest pinning the exact code state used. Required for reproducibility whenever PipelineSourceRepository is set. Example: '7a3b2c1d4e5f6a7b8c9d0e1f2a3b4c5d6e7f8a9b' or 'sha256:e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855'.

metadata_.format: git commit SHA (40 hex chars) or OCI image digest (sha256:64 hex chars)

description: Human-readable branch, tag, or release identifier pointing at the same code state as PipelineSourceCommit. Examples: 'v3.10.1', 'main', '2026.04-release', 'feature/dragen-4-2'. Provides analyst-friendly context when a commit SHA alone is opaque.

Container image and tag identifying the runtime environment in which the pipeline executed, when applicable. Captures the dependency closure that PipelineSourceCommit alone cannot: the same code may run differently against different system libraries. Examples: 'nfcore/rnaseq:3.10.1', 'broadinstitute/gatk:4.4.0.0', 'us-docker.pkg.dev/dragen/dragen:4.2.4'.

Pointer to the configuration or parameter set used for this pipeline run. DOI, URL, content hash, or relative path within PipelineSourceRepository. Captures the difference between two runs of the same code with different parameters — without this, two valid runs of the same pipeline producing different results are indistinguishable. Examples: 'configs/grch38_default.yaml', '10.5281/zenodo.12345', 'sha256:abc123...'.
